overview: Esper Midrange in Pioneer leverages efficient disruption and resilient threats to control the board while generating long-term value. By combining early interaction with mid-to-late game card advantage, the deck aims to outlast aggressive strategies and outvalue control decks. Success relies on precise sequencing of removal and disruption to protect key assets.
win_conditions: Disrupting key plays with Thoughtseize and Fatal Push
Generating card advantage with Quantum Riddler to outvalue decks

keep_criteria: Hands with early disruption like Thoughtseize or Fatal Push
Two-mana spells paired with a threat
Hands that can interact with aggressive starts
Keep hands with 2-3 lands and at least one early interaction (Thoughtseize or Fatal Push).
Prioritize hands that can play Thoughtseize on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Three-mana or higher spells without disruption
Hands with only lands or only threats
Mulligan to lands if you have fewer than two, or if your hand consists entirely of 3+ mana spells with no land/draw support.

early_game: Play lands and use Fatal Push to remove early threats
Cast Thoughtseize to disrupt opponent's game plan
Hold up mana for interaction if possible
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize if possible (target discard, removal, or early threat).
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Fatal Push on a 2-drop threat, or develop board with a midrange creature.
Land sequencing: Prioritize basics that support WUB
if playing fetch/shock lands, fetch for the right color and shock for tempo when needed.
mid_game: Deploy Quantum Riddler to generate card advantage
Trade efficiently with removal and disruption
Stabilize against aggressive decks while building board presence
Hoard removal for key threats and use Thoughtseize to disrupt opponent's hand before committing threats.
Manage mana to maintain flexibility between spells and threats.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without card advantage.
Commit threats when the opponent has no removal or sweepers.
Hold back if they have counterspells or sweepers in play or hand.
Use removal efficiently on tempo threats that disrupt your game plan.
late_game: Use Quantum Riddler's ability to outvalue control decks
Deploy resilient threats to close out games
Maintain hand advantage to outlast attrition strategies
Quantum Riddler combined with other midrange threats and card advantage.
Use discard and removal to clear the path, then close the game with resilient threats and card draw.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal or sweepers, or when you have a clear card advantage advantage.
Commit when you can safely develop your board without fear of being wiped.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Use to remove key combo pieces, early threats, or win conditions. Prioritize targeting cards that enable the opponent's strategy.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to clear 1-toughness threats. Hold up for combat tricks or to protect your own creatures when necessary.

key_cards.name: Quantum Riddler
key_cards.notes: Deploy as a resilient threat. Use its ability to draw cards and gain tempo, especially in longer games against control or midrange decks.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Fatal Push mana to protect Quantum Riddler from early removal
Cast Thoughtseize before playing threats to ensure they stick
Use Quantum Riddler's ability on opponent's turn to maximize card advantage
Common mistake: overcommitting to the board without card advantage or tracking opponent's removal.
Quantum Riddler's value scales with discard and removal synergy, so prioritize keeping it in hand.
Always track opponent's mana and hand size to predict their next move.
Avoid playing around cards that don't exist
focus on the current board state and known information.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Fatal Push on early threats and use Thoughtseize to delay their game plan. Play Quantum Riddler behind a blocker if possible.
Use Thoughtseize to remove key answers or win conditions. Deploy Quantum Riddler to outvalue their hand and generate pressure.
Target combo pieces with Thoughtseize immediately. Use Fatal Push to disrupt early setup creatures. Race with Quantum Riddler if disruption fails.
Use split effects, cast threats at end of turn, and use Thoughtseize to remove counterspells. Overcommit with value threats that can't be countered or that provide immediate impact.
Apply early disruption with Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. Don't let them cast big spells; answer threats efficiently and pressure before they stabilize.
Win the discard war by tracking their hand and removing key threats. Hold up removal for their threats and sweepers. Win with superior late-game threats and card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Doomskar), counterspells, and combo pieces.
Note when opponents tap out for threats versus holding up removal.
Track graveyard usage and discard patterns to predict their next move.
